rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATI0N,INC DBA GREAT WESTERN VS. SIMIN SIDDIQ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate The Order Granting Relief From Default And Default Judgment; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 24, 2012, Line 2. PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATI0N, INC DBA GREAT WESTERN Motion To Vacate The Order Granting Relief From Default And Default Judgment. Granted. The Court lacked jurisdiction to vacate the default on January 6, 2010 because defendant filed for bankruptcy prior to that time. Accordingly, the January 6, 2010 order is void and is vacated per Code of Civil Procedure 473(d). The six month deadline does not apply to a void order. The Court also strikes the answer filed on January 6, 2010. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted by the moving party. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK MALKASSIAN et al VS. HOWARD MULLIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Set Two And Special Interrogatories Set One
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 24, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS PATRICK MALKASSIAN and ELIZABETH NALBANDIAN's Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Set Two And Special Interrogatories Set One. Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiffs¿ Motion to Compel is Denied. Defendant Pacific Union Real Estate Group, Ltd. is a suspended corporation and as such, it is prohibited by law from engaging in any litigation, including discovery under California Corporations Code section 2205(c), Revenue & Tax Code section 23301 and Palm Valley Homeowners Assn., Inc. v. Design MTC (2000) 85 Cal.App.4th 553, 561. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to abarton@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CORZAC, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton To Strike And Special Motion To Strike "Slapp"
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 24, 2012, Line 24. DEFENDANT MONIQUE MOYER, THOMAS CARTER, SUSAN REYNOLDS, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO Motion To Strike And Special Motion To Strike "Slapp". Granted. The eighth cause of action for defamation arises from protected activity and Plaintiffs have not that the claim has the requisite minimal merit. on the claim. The statements made by Reynolds and Dunn-Martin are privileged under Civil Code section 47(a) and the statements of San Francisco Police Department personnel are immune from liability under Government Code sections 815.2 and 821.6. rulings.Case Title: SU MAH et al VS. JORDAN M ASSAF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 24, 2012, Line 10. DEFENDANT JORDAN ASSAF, BEN ASSAF MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Granted. Defendant Ben Asaaf satisfied his burden of production and Plaintiffs failed to raise a triable issue whether Ben Assaf negligently entrusted his vehicle to Jordan Assaf. The motion is not an improper attempt to adjudicate a legal issue subsumed within a cause of action. Although not pleased as a separate claim, negligent entrustment is treated as a separate claim for summary adjudication purposes since it is based on separate and distinct alleged wrongful conduct from the conduct supporting plaintiffs¿ other claims. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. JACKSON JENKINS RENSTROM LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 24, 2012, Line 13. DEFENDANT JACKSON JENKINS RENSTROM LLP MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Denied in its entirety. Defendant fails to demonstrate that the Side Agreement is void for a failure of consideration. Even if J&W never obtained the tail insurance, it provided a benefit to JJR by assigning the Fireman¿s Fund account to JJR. JJR also fails to demonstrate that the Side Agreement was ever rescinded. Plaintiff has not split its causes of actions. Plaintiff¿s claims against J&W are based on primary rights that are totally distinct from its claims against JJR. As to the third cause of action for breach of contract, while it is arguable whether plaintiff is an intended third party beneficiary of the Side Agreement, summary adjudication is denied per Code of Civil Procedure 437c(h). Deponents Jackson and Renstrom have information regarding the parties¿ intent when they executed the Side Agreement yet they failed to appear for their depositions without substantial justification. Defendant fails to satisfy its burden of production on the conversion claim that it is not retaining funds owed to plaintiff. Defendant also fails to satisfy its burden on the request for punitive damages because the request is supported by the conversion claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
